meaning: n.混合物,大杂烩,杂乱的一群,[纺]混色纱
examples: Using dual-constant Kubelka-Munk theory for colour matching of melange fibres is discussed.
Granted that a catalog is by its very nature a melange.
In the 2010 image, the same area is melange-broken glacier ice and sea ice covered with snow.
Yoakam draws a crazy-quilt melange of fans, from deep-woods traditional country diehards to coffee-sipping, Americana-loving hipsters.
The event is, like many aspects of post-colonial Mexico, a melange of influences.
